claim.content:
For a metallic system near a Mott metal-insulator transition with quasiparticle self-energy $\Sigma(\mathbf{k},\omega)$, the quasiparticle effective mass at the Fermi momentum $\mathbf{k}_F$ can be factorized as $\frac{m^*}{m_b}=\left(1-\left.\frac{\partial\mathrm{Re}\,\Sigma(\mathbf{k},\omega)}{\partial\omega}\right|_{\omega=E_F,\mathbf{k}_F}\right)\times\frac{|\partial_{\mathbf{k}}\varepsilon_{\mathbf{k}}^{0}|_{\mathbf{k}_F}}{|\partial_{\mathbf{k}}\varepsilon_{\mathbf{k}}^{0}|_{\mathbf{k}_F}+\left.\partial_{\mathbf{k}}\mathrm{Re}\,\Sigma(\mathbf{k},\omega)\right|_{\omega=E_F,\mathbf{k}_F}}$, i.e., $\frac{m^*}{m_b}= \frac{m_\omega}{m_b}\times\frac{m_k}{m_b}$. If the frequency-dependent part (the "$\omega$-mass" $m_\omega/m_b$) increases strongly (quasiparticle weight $Z\to0$) but the momentum-dependent term $\partial_{\mathbf{k}}\mathrm{Re}\,\Sigma$ is of the sign and magnitude needed to reduce the second ($k$-mass) factor significantly, then the product can remain only moderately enhanced; this mechanism can reconcile large spectroscopic suppression of spectral weight near $E_F$ with moderate thermodynamic/magnetic masses. Symbols: $m^*$ quasiparticle mass, $m_b$ band mass, $\varepsilon_{\mathbf{k}}^{0}$ noninteracting band dispersion, $\Sigma(\mathbf{k},\omega)$ self-energy, $E_F$ Fermi energy.
      Analytic decomposition and qualitative proposal discussed in the paper and related theoretical literature (Refs. 41,42).
